boundary_status_reviews.reasoning: Amazon deforestation declined but degradation exceeds clearing; rainfall decline accelerates dieback risk per Nature Communications. Stable trajectory does not justify downgrade despite policy improvements.

tipping_trajectory_assessments.reasoning: 9% methane emissions rise since 2002 with deep sediment double warming potential per Nature Geoscience. Tier 2 sources confirm continuation but lack Tier 1 for acceleration call.

analyst_briefing: Seven planetary boundaries remain beyond safe limits, with ocean acidification confirmed crossed (SRC Tier 1) and no recommended status changes. Tipping assessments show AMOC and coral accelerating (High confidence, PIK/AIMS Tier 1 validation); permafrost/Amazon continuation (Assessed, Tier 2). Ice sheets stable loss. New cascades: mass displacement escalation to 83.4M (IDMC Confirmed) flags SCEM; US drought/El Niño food insecurity to GMM. Escalations: SCEM (displacement/AMOC Africa); GMM (stranded assets); WDM (NDC gaps). AMOC 60% faster weakening and coral consecutive bleaching signal trajectory acceleration requiring priority tracking. No contested findings.
